---
<<<<<<< HEAD
<<<<<<< HEAD
title: "Reference: S3 methods"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Reference: S3 methods}
=======
=======
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
title: "S3 Methods Reference"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    code_folding: show
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{S3 Methods Reference}
<<<<<<< HEAD
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
=======
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
<<<<<<< HEAD
<<<<<<< HEAD
  comment = "#>",
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 4.5
)
library(nimble)
set.seed(1)
FAST <- TRUE
mcmc <- if (FAST) {
  list(niter = 300, nburnin = 80, thin = 2, nchains = 1, seed = 1)
} else {
  list(niter = 5000, nburnin = 1500, thin = 5, nchains = 2, seed = c(1, 2))
}
```

## Goal

This vignette is a quick reference for common S3 methods on a `mixgpd_fit` object.

## Fit a small model

```{r}
library(DPmixGPD)

y <- abs(rnorm(50)) + 0.1
bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(
  y = y,
  backend = "sb",
  kernel = "normal",
  GPD = TRUE,
  components = 6,
  mcmc = mcmc
)
fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE)
```

## `print()`

```{r}
print(fit)
```

## `summary()`

```{r}
summary(fit)
```

## `plot()`

```{r}
try(plot(fit, family = "trace"), silent = TRUE)
```

## `predict()`

```{r}
# Mean / median
predict(fit, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit
predict(fit, type = "median", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit

# Quantile
predict(fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.90, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit
```

## `fitted()`

```{r}
# Returns a data.frame with fit, interval, residuals
f <- fitted(fit, type = "mean", level = 0.90)
head(f)
```

## Object structure

```{r}
str(fit, max.level = 2)
```

```{r sessioninfo, include=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
=======
=======
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
  comment = NA,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  eval = FALSE
)
library(DPmixGPD)
```

# Core S3 methods

| Method | Object | Purpose | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| `print()` | `dpmixgpd_bundle` | quick bundle summary | concise configuration | 
| `summary()` | `dpmixgpd_bundle` | detailed bundle summary | lists code/data/monitors |
| `print()` | `mixgpd_fit` | fit summary | kernel/backends/mcmc info |
| `summary()` | `mixgpd_fit` | posterior summary | parameter tables |
| `plot()` | `mixgpd_fit` | diagnostics | trace, running mean, autocorrelation |
| `predict()` | `mixgpd_fit` | posterior predictive | density, quantile, mean, survival |

# Parameter naming conventions

Common parameter names in outputs:

- `w` (mixture weights)
- `alpha` (concentration)
- kernel parameters (e.g., `mean`, `sd`, `shape`, `scale`)
- link coefficients: `beta_<param>`
- GPD links: `beta_threshold`, `beta_tail_scale`

# Typical usage patterns

```{r s3-example}
fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle)
print(fit)
summary(fit)
plot(fit, family = c("traceplot", "running"))
q_pred <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", index = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))
plot(q_pred)
```

# Notes

- `predict()` returns a structured object with `fit` and interval summaries.
- `plot()` accepts families such as `traceplot`, `running`, and `autocorrelation`.
- Use `params()` for convenient access to posterior draws in original scales.
<<<<<<< HEAD
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
=======
>>>>>>> 50289162bd36853addda01bb01ee507dfa332090
